parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Limit for pagination purposes.

If unspecified or 0, the default value is 50. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Page token of the current page.

If not given or "", the first page is returned.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

get.description:
List [role]s of [subject]s.

> **Info:**
> Not only Users are [subject]s, but anything that can have roles attached. For example, Devices.

> **Info:**
> Modeled after [List role assignments](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/role-assignments-list-portal).

Might be filtered by the specified [subject] or [scope]s.

If the filters are left empty, all the roles available to the caller are returned.

Example:
Request*
```yaml
---
scopes:
- msp_uuid: af631cc9-3e9f-4fd7-8f29-ed8121b4cf8a
```

Response*
```yaml
---
assignments:
- subject_reference: 286f5456-a0ac-4e8a-8508-5c2224b47ae6
roles:
- role_name: admin
scopes:
- msp_uuid: af631cc9-3e9f-4fd7-8f29-ed8121b4cf8a
- customer_uuid: 30bd93aa-c0ef-4fcf-a73f-ce80610bd161
- role_name: auditor
scopes:
- msp_uuid: 30bd93aa-c0ef-4fcf-a73f-ce80610bd161
- subject_reference: ae9e45d2-9ee6-43e7-9b68-a650d62eeff1
roles:
- role_name: admin
scopes:
- msp_uuid: af631cc9-3e9f-4fd7-8f29-ed8121b4cf8a
- customer_uuid: ff572f63-8965-47da-9d9d-cb994dc9da10
- role_name: auditor
scopes:
- msp_uuid: af631cc9-3e9f-4fd7-8f29-ed8121b4cf8a
- customer_uuid: 30bd93aa-c0ef-4fcf-a73f-ce80610bd161
```

parameters.name: includeNestedScopes
parameters.description:
Scopes can be nested, and some scopes naturally exist within the boundaries of another scope.

If true, besides the requested scopes, nested scopes are also included in the response.

parameters.name: subjectReference
parameters.description:
Return only roles of the [subject](https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-3/sp800-63-3.html#subject)

Reference can be the User UUID, EPC instance UUID, or whatever identifies the subject.

If omitted, all the subjects (accessible to the caller) are returned.

> **Info:**
> `sub` claim can be used:
> [JSON Web Token Claims](https://auth0.com/docs/secure/tokens/json-web-tokens/json-web-token-claims#reserved-claims)
>
> Identical to the `principal` concept from the Microsoft literature: [Security principals](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/access-control/security-principals)

parameters.name: subjectType
parameters.description:
Type of the subject in reference.

 - SUBJECT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_USER: Subject represents a [user].
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_DEVICE: Subject represents a [device].
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_USER_GROUP: Roles can be assigned to a user group.

Then, the roles are inferred from the groups of the authenticated user.
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_MANAGED_IDENTITY: The subject has a managed identity.

Managed identities are used by the API clients to access services.
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_SERVICE: The subject represents a [service].

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Limit for pagination purposes.

If unspecified or 0, the default value is 50. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Page token of the current page.

If not given or "", the first page is returned.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of fields (from the request resource).

Allowed formats are:
- [snake_case version of the field name](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/style#message_and_field_names) as used by protocol buffers.
- [JSON version of the field name](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#json)

The syntax is described at [Standard Methods: List Ordering](https://google.aip.dev/132#ordering)

post.description:
Assign a role to a subject. Eventually, the subject ends with its existing roles and those assigned by this operation.

For example:
"User X" is an "admin" of the Subscription B. 
`AssignRole( "User X", { "admin", ["Subscription A", "Subscription C"]) `
leaves the user being an admin of the Subscriptions A, B, and C.

post.description:
Remove the assigned role from the subject. Eventually, the subject ends without roles or scopes unassigned by this operation.

For example:
"User X" is an "admin" of the Subscriptions A, B, C. 
`RevokeRole( "User X", { "admin", "Subscription B"}) `
leaves the user being an admin of the Subscriptions A and C.

> **Info:**

subjectReference.description: Reference to the [user], [device] or whatever identifies the subject.

nextPageToken.description:
Page token of the next page.

Empty or "" for the last page.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

Page token of the next page.

Empty or "" for the last page.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

totalSize.description:
The total count of items in the list irrespective of pagination.

> **Info:**
> One of the [standard fields](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/standard_fields)

`Page_size` might differ for every call (it is an input parameter) so the calculation of how many pages there is in total is caller's responsibility.

name.description:
Permission name. It is a unique ID of the permission.

It must be a URL-safe identifier. It must consist only of alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9), dashes (-), and underscores (_). Spaces and special characters are not allowed.

> **Info:**
> For EPC, the display name is formed by concatenating strings for functionality and possible actions (Read, Write, Use) in the format functionality.action, separated by the symbol '.'
> Example: Rogue Detection Sensor Database Reset.Read

v2Permission.description: Permission specifies the allowed actions that can be performed.

subjectReference.description: Reference to the [user], [device] or whatever identifies the subject.

description.description: A human-readable description of the role definition.

displayName.description: A human-readable name of the Role.

name.description:
The role name.
It is a unique ID of the role in the defined scope.

It must be a URL-safe identifier. It must consist only of alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9), dashes (-), and underscores (_). Spaces and special characters are not allowed.

permissionNames.description:
The names of the permissions this role grants.

Permissions are referenced by their name.

v2Role.description: A Role is a collection of permissions that define what actions can be performed.

assetGroupUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [group] from asset management.

type: asset_management.v1.Group

customerUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [customer].

> **Info:**
> The [customer] scope is covered by the more abstract [tenant] scope, which will eventually be used instead of the [customer] scope.

type: customer_management.v2.Customer

deviceUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [device].

type: device_management.v1.Device

mspUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [MSP].

> **Info:**
> The [MSP] scope is covered by the more abstract [tenant] scope, which will eventually be used instead of the [customer] scope.

type: msp_management.v1.Msp

policyUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [policy].

type: policy_management.v1.Policy

subscriptionUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [subscription].

type: subscription_management.v1.Subscription

userUuid.description:
Scope of the referenced [user].

type: user_management.v1.User

tenantUuid.description:
Tenant typically represents an organization unit recognized by ESET.

Tenant defines the logical scope of the managed entities. Each managed entity is owned by exactly one tenant, which means its life cycle is inherently dependent on the tenant’s life cycle.

type: tenant_management.v1.Tenant

v2Scope.description:
[Scope] for a role assignment. Scope narrows down the application of a role.

[Scope]s can be hierarchical, which implies role inheritance.

> **Info:**
> On the Internet the concept of the scope (for example, [Scope](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/scope-overview)) can be found under different names:
>
> `Condition` from:
> [REST Resource: roleAssignments](https://developers.google.com/admin-sdk/directory/reference/rest/v1/roleAssignments)
>
> `Context` from:
> [Term Definition: Contextual Role](http://www.unified-am.com/UAM/UAM/guidances/termdefinitions/uam_context-role_B9C4F862.html)

roleName.description:
The role name is the identifier of the Role.

For example, "admin", or "security.viewer", or  "2a2b9908-6ea1-4ae2-8e65-a410df84e7d1"

Roles are referenced by their names.

scopes.description:
Map of [role]s to [scope]s.

If the [scope] is left empty, the role is considered global for all the [scope]s.

The first string stands for the role (for example, "Auditor").

v2ScopedRole.description:
Combines the [role] and [scope]s. Roles might have effects limited by the scopes. For example, a user might be an admin of a specific device.

> **Info:**
> On the Internet the concept of the scope (for example, [Scope](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/scope-overview)) can be found under different names:
>
> `Condition` from:
> [REST Resource: roleAssignments](https://developers.google.com/admin-sdk/directory/reference/rest/v1/roleAssignments)
>
> `Context` from:
> [Term Definition: Contextual Role](http://www.unified-am.com/UAM/UAM/guidances/termdefinitions/uam_context-role_B9C4F862.html)

roles.description: List of [role]s with their respective scopes.

subjectReference.description:
Reference of the [subject](https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-3/sp800-63-3.html#subject).

> **Info:**
> Identical to the `principal` concept from the Microsoft literature: [Security principals](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/access-control/security-principals)

v2SubjectType.description:
The type of principal represented by the subject to whom the roles are assigned.

 - SUBJECT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_USER: Subject represents a [user].
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_DEVICE: Subject represents a [device].
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_USER_GROUP: Roles can be assigned to a user group.

Then, the roles are inferred from the groups of the authenticated user.
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_MANAGED_IDENTITY: The subject has a managed identity.

Managed identities are used by the API clients to access services.
 - SUBJECT_TYPE_SERVICE: The subject represents a [service].

Bearer.description: Type "Bearer " and then your API Token
Bearer.name: Authorization
